issue: No issue found - demo already working correctly
root_cause: Demo properly configured with clipping planes
fix: No fix needed - clipping setup is correct

fix_details.clipping_setup: Two clipping planes (plane1 and plane2) with Plane and Vector3
fix_details.material_properties: clippingPlanes and clipShadows properly set on MeshStandardMaterial

verification.visual_check: Demo shows red torus knot being clipped by horizontal plane with shadow on ground

additional_notes: The demo uses JSX approach with Canvas3D, proper OrbitControls setup, and shadows enabled. Clipping plane visualization is implicit through the clipped geometry.
kimi_reasoning: Image 1 (PORTED) displays a web interface with a dark sidebar navigation panel (@woby/three) listing examples like 'Plane' and '3 Boxes + Click', while the main viewport shows a black background with large red curved shapes on the left and right edges, resembling abstract background graphics or a different scene entirely. It lacks the interactive 3D content, floor plane, and lighting setup. Image 2 (REFERENCE) shows the standard Three.js 'clipping planes' example featuring a light gray floor plane receiving shadows, a central red geometric object (appearing as clipped planar sections with wireframe edges), a black background, an FPS counter in the top-left, and a 'Controls' panel in the top-right for 'Local Clipping' and 'Global Clipping' settings. The ported version completely fails to render the actual 3D scene content, missing the floor, the clipped object, shadows, and all UI controls present in the reference.
kimi_key_differences: Missing floor plane: Reference has a light gray ground plane receiving shadows; Ported has no floor
Missing central object: Reference shows a red clipped geometric structure in the center; Ported shows only abstract red curved shapes on screen edges
Missing shadow rendering: Reference shows clear object shadows on the floor; Ported has no shadows
Missing UI controls: Reference includes a 'Controls' panel with clipping checkboxes; Ported has a sidebar navigation menu instead
Missing FPS counter: Reference displays '60 FPS' in top-left corner; Ported has no performance indicator
Different layout: Reference is a fullscreen canvas; Ported splits screen with sidebar and main viewport
